nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
**Webhook from Line**

















You need to set-up this webhook at Line Manager or Line Developer Console

You'll need to copy Webhook URL from this node to put in Line Console

Also, don't forget to remove 'test' part when going for production

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
**Line Loading Animation**

















This node is to only give ... loading animation back in Line.

It seems stupid but it actually tells user that the workflow is running and you are not left waiting without hope

To authorize, you can fill in the Line Token in the node here, or you can you header authorization (shown at the 'reply message' node)

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

This is to call LLMs model at Openrouter.ai 

You can use it to call ChatGPT, Lllama, Qwen, Deepseek, and much more with just standardized API call and top-up only once

{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: Chinese Translator. This workflow integrates 6 different services: webhook, stickyNote, httpRequest, switch, stopAndError. It contains 40 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Chinese Translator. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

parameters.content:
**OpenRouter.ai**

















This is to call LLMs model at Openrouter.ai 

You can use it to call ChatGPT, Lllama, Qwen, Deepseek, and much more with just standardized API call and top-up only once

{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

**Webhook from Line**

















You need to set-up this webhook at Line Manager or Line Developer Console

You'll need to copy Webhook URL from this node to put in Line Console

Also, don't forget to remove 'test' part when going for production

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

**Line Loading Animation**

















This node is to only give ... loading animation back in Line.

It seems stupid but it actually tells user that the workflow is running and you are not left waiting without hope

To authorize, you can fill in the Line Token in the node here, or you can you header authorization (shown at the 'reply message' node)

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}
## You can test this workflow by adding Line @405jtfqs
**Router for Text, Image, Voice, and others

**Line Reply**
Similar to above but from different route

**OpenRouter.ai**
We will use image as prompt and change the model to support image.

**Pre-processing**














We need to use get media API to get the data from Line and also move it to base64 file to prompt

**Line Reply**
To reply that message is not supported

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Line Webhook".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "Line Loading Animation" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Image" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenRouter : qwen/qwen2.5-vl-72b-instruct:free" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenRouter: qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct:free" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Extract from File" node (n8n-nodes-base.extractFromFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note9" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Line Reply (image)" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Line Reply (Text)" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Line Reply (Not Supported 2)" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
